page_content:
PHYS476 Introduction to Applied Machine Learning (3 Credits) Introduces machine learning techniques that are becoming pertinent in the technology industry. Focus on hands-on work using popular high- level libraries. Students are expected to have a background in functional programming, linear algebra, calculus, and mathematical modeling. Prerequisite: PHYS165, PHYS274, and PHYS276; or interested students with backgrounds in functional programming, linear algebra and statistics, should contact the instructors to request permission.

PHYS485 Electronic Circuits (3 Credits) Theory and application to experimental physics of modern semiconductor analog and digital circuits. Emphasis on understanding passive and active elements in practical circuits. Topics span the range from simple transistor circuits to microcomputers. Prerequisite: PHYS272 and PHYS276. Restriction: Must be in a major within CMNS-Physics department.

PHYS603 Methods of Statistical Physics (3 Credits) Foundations and applications of thermodynamics and statistical mechanics. Credit Only Granted for: PHYS602 or PHYS603.

PHYS604 Methods of Mathematical Physics (3 Credits) Ordinary and partial differential equations of physics, boundary value problems, Fourier series, Green's functions, complex variables and contour integration. Prerequisite: Must have completed coursework in advanced calculus; and (PHYS411 and PHYS410). Or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department.
